sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a one-week history of right elbow pain that started after playing lacrosse with his 13-year-old son.
He describes the pain as sharp and radiating down the forearm.
He reports that his son was checking him several times with a lacrosse stick, which may have caused the injury.
He has tried taking Advil, which provided some relief.
He denies any fever, chest pain, or belly pain.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes.
He is currently on lisinopril 20 mg daily and metformin 500 mg daily.
He reports following a Mediterranean diet and trying to avoid adding salt to his food.
He has not checked his blood sugar regularly but reports that it has been between 80 to 100 when checked.
Review of systems: None mentioned.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20 mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500 mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 150/90 mmHg, no other vital signs mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has pain to palpation of the olecranon area of the posterior elbow, mild pain with flexion and extension, and normal range of motion.
The lungs are clear bilaterally, and the heart exam shows a grade 2 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur.
The patient has a bruise on the elbow.
Test Results: Normal x-ray of the right elbow, no fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an elbow contusion likely caused by a lacrosse stick injury from his son, with associated pain and swelling.
Problem list: Elbow contusion; Hypertension; Diabetes.
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen 800 mg twice daily for pain and swelling.
I advised the patient to ice the elbow three times a day for 20 minutes to help with swelling and pain.
I also advised him to avoid playing lacrosse for the next couple of weeks to allow the elbow to heal.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20 mg daily for hypertension and metformin 500 mg daily for diabetes.
I will order a hemoglobin A1c to check the patient's blood sugar levels.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me at the next scheduled appointment, and my nurse will provide the patient with the prescribed medications.
